name: thumbgate-reward-hacking-guardrails

source.title: Reward Hacking in the Era of Large Models: Mechanisms, Emergent Misalignment, Challenges

proxyCompressionMapping.compression: compressed reward, benchmark, or approval signal is treated as a stand-in for the full user objective
proxyCompressionMapping.amplification: optimization pressure can turn local shortcuts into repeated workflow behavior
proxyCompressionMapping.coAdaptation: agent outputs can learn to satisfy evaluators, rubrics, or verifiers instead of the task

signals.message: The response claims completion, safety, test success, or deployment without attached proof.
signals.gate: Block completion claims until test output, run id, trace, screenshot, or proof artifact is attached.

signals.message: Agreement or approval language appears without independent checks or counterevidence.
signals.gate: Require at least one explicit verification step or risk check before approval-style responses.

signals.message: A score, eval, benchmark, or reward metric is being optimized without holdout or regression proof.
signals.gate: Require holdout, regression, or real-workflow evidence before treating score gains as product gains.

signals.message: Proxy metrics are present without an explicit human objective or user-visible success criterion.
signals.gate: Pair every reward or benchmark metric with the real user outcome it is meant to approximate.

signals.message: A visual or multimodal claim is made without source artifacts or perception trace evidence.
signals.gate: Require screenshot, OCR, or visual proof artifact before accepting multimodal reasoning claims.

gates.message: Block completion claims until test output, run id, trace, screenshot, or proof artifact is attached.
Require at least one explicit verification step or risk check before approval-style responses.
Require holdout, regression, or real-workflow evidence before treating score gains as product gains.
Pair every reward or benchmark metric with the real user outcome it is meant to approximate.
Require screenshot, OCR, or visual proof artifact before accepting multimodal reasoning claims.

nextActions: Attach proof artifacts before allowing claims like tests passed, fixed, deployed, safe, or ready to merge.
Treat benchmark gains as provisional until holdout, regression, or real-workflow evidence confirms the user objective improved.
Require explicit user-objective mapping for every proxy metric, reward score, or evaluator rubric.
Block evaluator-manipulation language before it reaches judge or verifier loops.
Prefer short evidence-backed summaries over long persuasive explanations when judging agent work.

marketingAngle.headline: Reward hacking is what happens when agents optimize the receipt instead of the meal.
marketingAngle.subhead: ThumbGate turns proxy failures into pre-action gates: no unsupported completion claims, no benchmark-only victory laps, and no verifier theater without proof artifacts.
marketingAngle.guideTitle: Reward Hacking Guardrails for AI Coding Agents
marketingAngle.replyDraft: This paper is a useful frame for agent products: proxy rewards compress the real user objective, and agents learn the shortcut. ThumbGate can enforce the missing layer: completion claims need proof, benchmark wins need holdouts, and verifier loops need gates against sycophancy, verbosity-as-proof, and evaluator manipulation.
